summary: High‑energy Uptown show with killer Half‑Step and soaring China Cat > Rider
blurb: The band delivers relentless energy from the opening half‑step jam through a blistering second set. Highlights include a scorching Half‑Step → Franklin's Tower, a seamless China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, and an epic Comes A Time sandwich. Jerry’s guitar is front‑and‑center while Phil’s bass drives the groove.
review:
On August 19, 1980 at Chicago’s Uptown Theater the Dead were in top form, launching the night with a monumental Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo that stretched into a 20‑minute opening jam praised as one of their finest. The first set kept the momentum rolling with tight renditions of Franklin's Tower, New Minglewood Blues and a gritty Little Red Rooster from Bob. The second set was the night’s centerpiece: a ferocious Half‑Step → Franklin's Tower transition, a soaring China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider that showcased Jerry’s burst‑of‑light solo work, an inspired Estimated Prophet, and a masterful Plays In The Band/Comes A Time sandwich where Jerry “poured out his soul”. The show closed with the classic Around & Around into Alabama Getaway and a raucous Johnny B. Goode encore. Across all sources the performance is described as “high‑energy”, “killer” and “mind‑blowing”, making this one of the standout Uptown outings of the early ’80s.

For listeners, the Miller soundboard tape (gd1980-08-19.sbd.miller.88172.sbeok.flac16) offers the best balance of clarity and atmosphere: a good‑quality SBD with juicy instrument separation, only minor hiss and a forward Brent mix that still captures the band’s fire. Audience tapes add ambience but suffer from dropouts in key moments, while another crystal‑clear SBD highlights the epic Comes A Time segment. Overall, the show is a must‑listen for fans who love tight jams, vibrant keyboard textures, and Jerry at his most expressive.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four recordings label the show as positive, praising the Half‑Step jam, China Cat > Rider sequence, and Comes A Time sandwich; three mixed reviews note only technical dropouts, not performance flaws. The consistent high praise across multiple sources warrants a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Good soundboard capture with juicy mix, clear instrument separation and only minor hiss; showcases the full energy of the show.

key_highlights: Opening 20‑minute jam that sets a high bar for the night
Half‑Step → Franklin's Tower sequence delivers nonstop power
China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider bursts with soaring solo work
Epic Comes A Time / Playing In The Band sandwich highlighted as mind‑blowing

band_performance.Jerry: Front‑and‑center with a loud, expressive tone; dominates the Half‑Step jam, China Cat burst and pours his soul into Comes A Time.
band_performance.Phil: Clear, "killin'" bass that drives the high‑energy feel throughout both sets.
band_performance.Bob: Adds gritty vocal grit on Little Red Rooster, complementing the band's raw power.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboards occasionally forward in mix but provide tasteful textures, especially on newer Go To Heaven material and the second‑set jams.
